news.title:
Polycab and KEI Industries shares fall up to 7% over weak demand concerns.
Growth worries impact both companies as demand remains subdued.
news.summary:
Polycab and KEI Industries shares fall up to 7% over weak demand concerns.
Growth worries impact both companies as demand remains subdued.

news.title:
Jefferies maintains 'Buy' on Polycab, raises TP to Rs 9485.
Previous target price for Polycab was Rs 9225.
news.summary:
Jefferies maintains 'Buy' on Polycab, raises TP to Rs 9485.
Previous target price for Polycab was Rs 9225.

news.title:
Polycab faces Rs. 327.4 Cr tax demand; Rs. 41.9 Cr expenses disallowed for FY23-24.
Company plans rectification and appeal; expects no material financial impact.
news.summary:
Polycab faces Rs. 327.4 Cr tax demand; Rs. 41.9 Cr expenses disallowed for FY23-24.
Company plans rectification and appeal; expects no material financial impact.

news.title:
Polycab India Ltd's amalgamation of Uniglobus Electricals approved by NCLT Ahmedabad.
Scheme effective upon filing certified NCLT order with Registrar of Companies, Ahmedabad.
news.summary:
Polycab India Ltd's amalgamation of Uniglobus Electricals approved by NCLT Ahmedabad.
Scheme effective upon filing certified NCLT order with Registrar of Companies, Ahmedabad.

news.title:
8.6 lakh shares of Polycab India were traded on Tuesday.
IRCTC recorded losses for the second consecutive day.
news.summary:
8.6 lakh shares of Polycab India were traded on Tuesday.
IRCTC recorded losses for the second consecutive day.

news.title:
Morgan Stanley's picks include Tata Steel, Shriram Finance, Polycab, Grasim, Indian Oil, and Eternal, while cautioning on SBI Cards, SAIL, and SRF.
Jefferies highlights AU Small Finance Bank, Axis Bank, Bajaj Finance, Godrej Properties, Hindustan Zinc, JSW Energy, Bharat Forge, and Voltas.
news.summary:
Morgan Stanley's picks include Tata Steel, Shriram Finance, Polycab, Grasim, Indian Oil, and Eternal, while cautioning on SBI Cards, SAIL, and SRF.
Jefferies highlights AU Small Finance Bank, Axis Bank, Bajaj Finance, Godrej Properties, Hindustan Zinc, JSW Energy, Bharat Forge, and Voltas.

news.title:
US tariffs have hurt Indian cable and telecom firms like Sterlite Tech, HFCL, Polycab, Havells, and RR Kabel, impacting margins and exports amid a US data centre boom.
Companies aim to mitigate tariffs by localizing production or diversifying markets; demand remains strong in the US for data centres and broadband infrastructure.
news.summary:
US tariffs have hurt Indian cable and telecom firms like Sterlite Tech, HFCL, Polycab, Havells, and RR Kabel, impacting margins and exports amid a US data centre boom.
Companies aim to mitigate tariffs by localizing production or diversifying markets; demand remains strong in the US for data centres and broadband infrastructure.

news.title:
Polycab India reports 46% YoY revenue growth in Q3 FY26, driven by strong performance in Wires & Cables (W&C) and FMEG segments. PAT grew 36% YoY to INR 6.3 billion.
Institutional sales outpaced distributor sales; wires grew faster than cables due to higher copper prices. Export growth was 5% YoY, with Middle East and Latin America contributing strongly.
news.summary:
Polycab India reports 46% YoY revenue growth in Q3 FY26, driven by strong performance in Wires & Cables (W&C) and FMEG segments. PAT grew 36% YoY to INR 6.3 billion.
Institutional sales outpaced distributor sales; wires grew faster than cables due to higher copper prices. Export growth was 5% YoY, with Middle East and Latin America contributing strongly.

news.title:
Polycab India plans a 2-4% price hike from Jan 1 due to new BEE norms.
Export EBITDA margins at 15% surpass domestic margins of 9-12% (cables) and 15-16% (wires).
news.summary:
Polycab India plans a 2-4% price hike from Jan 1 due to new BEE norms.
Export EBITDA margins at 15% surpass domestic margins of 9-12% (cables) and 15-16% (wires).

news.title:
Polycab India expects strong Q4 performance driven by demand in govt and private sectors.
Solar business, leading in FMEG, anticipates robust growth in Q4 and future years.
news.summary:
Polycab India expects strong Q4 performance driven by demand in govt and private sectors.
Solar business, leading in FMEG, anticipates robust growth in Q4 and future years.

news.title:
Polycab India plans to raise advertising spend to 3-5% of B2C revenue.
Margins may face minor short-term impact due to rising commodity prices.
news.summary:
Polycab India plans to raise advertising spend to 3-5% of B2C revenue.
Margins may face minor short-term impact due to rising commodity prices.

news.title:
Polycab India's EPC business anticipates stable high single-digit margins mid-to-long term.
The international segment projects growth, backed by a robust order book.
news.summary:
Polycab India's EPC business anticipates stable high single-digit margins mid-to-long term.
The international segment projects growth, backed by a robust order book.

news.title:
Polycab India targets 1.5-2x industry growth for FMEG by FY30.
The company also aims for EBITDA margins of 8-10% by FY30 in FMEG.
news.summary:
Polycab India targets 1.5-2x industry growth for FMEG by FY30.
The company also aims for EBITDA margins of 8-10% by FY30 in FMEG.

news.summary: Morgan Stanley retains Overweight rating on Polycab, increasing target price to 9,659.

Audio recording of the call has been uploaded on the company's website as per SEBI regulations.

The presentation highlights product segments, growth, market leadership, and future strategies.

news.title:
Polycab India approved unaudited Q3 and 9-month results ending Dec 31, 2025.
Key directors reappointed; EPC business to be a separate segment from March 2025.

news.title:
Polycab India saw an NSE block trade worth Rs. 562.2 crore for ~764,902 shares.
The shares were traded at Rs. 7,350.00 each during the transaction.
